a:r.a:t: Conflicting incentives across Marketing, Telemarketing, and Sales (volume vs. quality) drive poor handoffs, lower conversion, and suboptimal funnel performance.

Excessive commission tiers and measure counts on some plans increase administrative complexity and dilute seller / marketer focus; targets changing monthly also reduces seller trust and ability to earn
Site-level plan variations and continued separation of Bluegreen / HGV plan design drive complexity and reduce consistency
Poor ramp up support for new hires
Inconsistent ramp-up support for early-tenure sales roles (especially Action Line), leads to low pay and high attrition within the first 6-12 months.

Heavy reliance on variable pay and income volatility (especially for early tenure sellers) increases attrition risk, makes roles less competitive, and shifts focus on short-term outcomes over sustained performance.
Lack of visible progression paths and differentiated role economics reduces retention of strong performers and pushes experienced talent to seek growth opportunities outside HGV.
Site-specific planning, target-setting approaches, and operational nuances (e.g., equitable rep rotations) create inconsistency and increase administrative burden.
Unclear decision ownership across cross-functional teams drives inconsistent plan designs, and limits enterprise-level control across HGV and Bluegreen.
Fragmented data ecosystem and source of truth
Disconnected systems and manually stitched data limit insight generation and lack a single source of truth for compensation performance

Disconnected data and spreadsheet-driven processes create material risk of errors, reduced trust in outputs, limit transparency and auditability, and create excessive delays in compensation reporting.
We identified 10 operating model gaps spread across compensation design, employee experience, governance, and supporting technology
Source of Insights: Based on internal leadership interviews, external competitor interviews, surveys, and plan/data review
